content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are competing against each other in a programming contest. They were both able to solve all the problems in the contest, so the winner between them must be decided by time penalty. Chef solved all the problems in XXX minutes and made PPP wrong submissions, while Chefina solved all the problems in YYY minutes and made QQQ wrong submissions. Who won the competition (or was it a draw)?

**Note:** The time penalty is calculated as follows:


The base time penalty is the time at which the final problem was solved (so, XXX for Chef and YYY for Chefina)
Each wrong submission adds a penalty of 101010 minutes
The winner is the person with less penalty time. If they both have the same penalty, it is considered a draw.

Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers — X,Y,PX,Y,PX,Y,P, and QQQ respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one string — the name of the winner ("Chef" or "Chefina"), or "Draw" if the match was a draw. Print each string without the quotes.


Each character of the answer can be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings "Chef", "chEf", "cHeF", etc. are treated as equivalent.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111:

Chef solved the problems in 101010 minutes and made 222 wrong submissions, thus his total penalty is 10+2⋅10=3010 + 2 \cdot 10 = 3010+2⋅10=30 minutes.
Chefina solved the problems in 888 minutes and made 333 wrong submissions, thus her total penalty is 8+3⋅10=388 + 3 \cdot 10 = 388+3⋅10=38 minutes.

Chef took less time, so he is the winner.
Test Case 222:

Chef solved the problems in 101010 minutes and made 101010 wrong submissions, thus his total penalty is 10+10⋅10=11010 + 10 \cdot 10 = 11010+10⋅10=110 minutes.
Chefina solved the problems in 101010 minutes and made 333 wrong submissions, thus her total penalty is 10+10⋅10=11010 + 10 \cdot 10 = 11010+10⋅10=110 minutes.

Chef and Chefina took the same time, so the match is a draw.
Test Case 333:

Chef solved the problems in 404040 minutes and made 222 wrong submissions, thus his total penalty is 40+2⋅10=6040 + 2 \cdot 10 = 6040+2⋅10=60 minutes.
Chefina solved the problems in 212121 minutes and made 000 wrong submissions, thus her total penalty is 21+0⋅10=2121 + 0 \cdot 10 = 2121+0⋅10=21 minutes.

Chefina took less time, so she is the winner.

code.C++:
# cook your dish here
t = int(input())

for i in range(t):
    x,y,p,q = map(int, input().split())
    
    penalty_x = p*10
    penalty_y = q*10
    
    total_x = x + penalty_x
    total_y = y + penalty_y
    
    if total_x < total_y:
        print('Chef')
    elif total_x > total_y:
        print('Chefina')
    elif total_x == total_y:
        print('Draw')
